source_pr.title: *: use pprof profile to collect CPU time group by SQL and plan digest
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

Track issue: #24875

### What problem does this PR solve?

This PR uses `pprof profile labels` to collect CPU time group by SQL and plan digest.

### What is changed and how it works?

The implementation is:

1: Use SQL digest and plan digest as the pprof labels.

```go
// After parse and calculated SQL digest, set the sql digest label
ctx = pprof.WithLabels(ctx, pprof.Labels("sql_digest", sql_digest))
pprof.SetGoroutineLabels(ctx)

...

// After the optimizer generated plan, set the plan digest label.
ctx = pprof.WithLabels(ctx, pprof.Labels("plan_digest", plan_digest))
pprof.SetGoroutineLabels(ctx)
```

There is a special case, for `execute stmt` SQL, should use the prepare SQL digest as the `sql_digest` pprof label.

2. Start 2 background worker to fetch profile and analyze the profile data.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26020263/119510499-25b77b80-bda4-11eb-8d3d-d89d60e8b72e.png)

Using a buffer channel to avoid parse and output take too much time that infect the fetching profile work.

#### Other problem

Because the `profile worker` keep calling `pprof.StartCPUProfile` to fetch profile data, other place (such pprof profile HTTP API handler) call `pprof.StartCPUProfile` will be failed.

In order to solve this problem, other place should fetch profile data from `profile worker` instead of `pprof.StartCPUProfile`.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

Side effects

- Performance regression
    - Consumes more CPU
    - Consumes more MEM

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- No release note

root_cause_explanation: The tracecpu/TopSQL profile test relied on `time.Sleep` and had unsynchronized shared state (mock collector maps + toggling global config), which could both cause timing flakiness under load and trigger data races under `-race`. The fix replaces `Sleep` with a bounded wait for an expected collect count, updates global config via `config.StoreGlobalConfig`, and protects collector state with mutex/atomic counters.
fix_pattern: Replace `time.Sleep` with a bounded wait for expected collection progress, use `config.StoreGlobalConfig` for config toggles, and protect mock collector state with mutex/atomic.
